top_candidates.reasoning: The 'imperishable disrepute' framing — explicitly named as akīrti avyayā in the analog, with the mirror to the imperishable-Self of 2.17 surfaced — is the structural signature of akīrtiṁ cāpi bhūtāni kathayiṣyanti te 'vyayām. The sambhāvitasya restriction is preserved as 'sambhāvitasya cākīrtir maraṇād atiricyate: the asymmetry is not vanity; it is the actual model of her that other operators will use.'
Adjacent verse — also reputation. But 2.35 sharpens to mahā-rathas (specific peer-class); 2.34 is about bhūtāni (all beings). Different scoping.
Same loss chain — names kīrti loss. But 2.33's loss is part of the if-then trio with svadharma and pāpa; 2.34 develops the kīrti loss alone into avyayām akīrtim.
